en.extract:
The Ibera seedeater (Sporophila iberaensis) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae, formerly included within the family of American sparrows (Emberizidae).
It is found in the Iberá Wetlands. It was described in 2016.


Taxonomy
The specific epithet iberaensis refers to the species' main distribution throughout the Iberá Wetlands in the province of Corrientes, Argentina.

The '''Ibera seedeater''' (''Sporophila iberaensis'') is a species of bird in the family [[Thraupidae]], formerly included within the family of American sparrows ([[Emberizidae]]).

It is found in the [[Iberá Wetlands]]. It was described in 2016.<ref name=":0">{{cite journal|last1=Di Giacomo |first1=Adrián Santiago |last2= Kopuchian|first2= Cecilia|date=2016|title=Una nueva especie de capuchino (Sporophila: Thraupidae) de los Esteros del Iberá, Corrientes, Argentina. |journal=Nuestras Aves |volume=61|pages=3–5 |access-date=2016-12-09|url= https://archive.org/details/RevistaNuestrasAves61Capuchino}}</ref>

== Taxonomy ==
The specific epithet ''iberaensis'' refers to the species' main distribution throughout the [[Iberá Wetlands]] in the [[Corrientes Province|province of Corrientes]], Argentina.
